title: #architecture: 1768003300
preview:
Talia Benson: Hi folks — need architecture eyes on the Shadow Traffic routing ADR before we mark it “accepted”. Can you review and leave comments inline by EOD tomorrow? Main things we need clarity on: *where* the mirroring happens in the request path + expected perf impact.

Talia Benson: Confluence ADR draft: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ARCH/ADR-Shadow-Traffic-Routing-Architecture (WIP)

Ravi Menon: Read through. Overall direction makes sense (inline mirroring, fail-open). I think the ADR needs to be explicit about placement:
1) request enters router
2) auth/tenant resolution
3) rollout variant assignment (control)
4) return control response to user
5) mirror *in parallel* to shadow target(s)

Ravi Menon: Right now section 2.1 kind of implies we might mirror before variant assignment; that would make comparing control vs shadow messy.

Mina Haddad: +1 to Ravi. Also: mirroring should happen after request normalization (canonicalized request payload) so fingerprinting is stable + we don’t end up hashing raw headers w/ PII.

Priya Natarajan: Skimmed. I’m aligned on inline mirroring as default vs async replay, but the ADR needs stronger justification on *performance risk*. Specifically:
- additional CPU in the hot path (hashing + serialization)
- additional network fanout and connection pool pressure
- any extra allocations around streaming

Priya Natarajan: Can we quantify overhead targets in the ADR (even rough): e.g., p99 added router CPU < X%, added latency on user path ~0ms (since fire-and-forget), and hard drop conditions.

Ethan Park: We can add a perf budget section. Implementation plan we’re using is: sampling decision happens early, and if not sampled we do basically nothing besides compute fingerprint (which we already do for rollout bucketing).

Ethan Park: For sampled requests, we spawn an async task with strict limits (max inflight per route + global circuit breaker). User path never awaits the shadow call.

Sean Gallagher: SRE view: the ADR should explicitly state what “fail-open” means at runtime. e.g.
- shadow target call timeout: 1.5s (or lower)
- if shadow queue depth > N OR event ingestion lag > M -> disable shadowing automatically (circuit breaker)
- if router is under load -> shadowing is first thing to shed

Sean Gallagher: Also I’d like it to call out that shadow errors are “acceptable” up to some threshold without paging (ticket vs page), so folks don’t assume shadow 99.9% success is required.

Allison Grant: +1. Also add a note about metrics cardinality. If router emits per-request tags, we’ll blow up Prometheus/OTel. The ADR should reference the companion data model ADR and explicitly say we only emit low-card dimensions.

Caleb Johnson: On perf: if we do inline mirroring, do we plan to emit an event on the control path (request start/end) AND a separate shadow pair event later? Need clarity on how we join control+shadow without storing huge payloads in the router.

Jordan Lee: Re: join key, in the schema thread we agreed on a deterministic `request_fingerprint_v1` + a per-request `shadow_pair_id` (or request_id) that’s safe to emit. Suggest ADR says: join happens in telemetry pipeline, not in router memory.

Jordan Lee: Also the ADR should explicitly specify the hashing input. I don’t want “implementation-defined” here. Proposed:
- canonicalized request body (excluding headers)
- route_id + tenant_id
- HMAC w/ server secret
Then truncate to 128 bits.

Mina Haddad: +1 but be careful: request body can be huge (multi-message chat). We should specify we hash a bounded representation: e.g., stable hash over first N bytes + lengths + model params. Otherwise we create CPU spikes.

Ravi Menon: Agree. For bucketing we don’t need cryptographic perfection, just stable-enough and privacy safe. HMAC is good. Also call out collision expectations and that collisions just add noise, not correctness bugs.

Benji Okafor: FYI we already made some policy decisions in eng-platform (schema review). Shadow policy defaults to consistent hashing using `request_fingerprint_v1`; no custom hash keys. Fail-open only (v1). Default `max_inflight_per_route=100` server-side. Might be useful to incorporate those into the ADR so it doesn’t read like “anything goes”.

Priya Natarajan: Great. Another question: where exactly is the mirror executed? In the same router process? separate threadpool? sidecar? The ADR says “routing layer” but that’s ambiguous.

Ethan Park: Current plan is router process (same binary), but separate “shadow executor” threadpool + bounded queue. When queue is full we drop. No backpressure to user path.

Sean Gallagher: Please spell out: bounded queue, bounded concurrency, and “drop” semantics (including counters). Without that, folks will keep adding retries and we’ll self-DDOS.

Allison Grant: Also: we should state whether the shadow call uses the same egress path and connection pools as the user call. If shared, we need a per-pool cap so shadowing doesn’t starve the control traffic under ephemeral port pressure.

Ravi Menon: Good call. ADR can say: shadow egress uses a dedicated client with lower priority + smaller pool + separate timeouts.

Caleb Johnson: Streaming: do we mirror streaming requests at all? If we do “mirror full prompt at start” that’s fine, but mirroring the stream tokens might double traffic and create weird partial captures.

Mina Haddad: For v1, I think the ADR should say: we mirror the *input* (request body) and capture the *shadow output* as non-streaming in the background (i.e., worker consumes full response). For user path, we keep streaming unaffected.

Jordan Lee: There’s still the issue that some request types depend on streaming partial tool calls, etc. But for shadow eval we mostly need final output. We can be explicit that shadow capture may be truncated/partial under timeouts and scoring must handle “incomplete”.

Priya Natarajan: +1: make degraded behavior explicit. The ADR should say what happens when:
- shadow target returns 429/5xx
- shadow target exceeds timeout
- scoring pipeline is down
- telemetry ingestion is lagging

Sean Gallagher: And importantly: none of those should page oncall unless control path is impacted. Shadow path pages should be separate and higher threshold.

Ethan Park: Action items I’m hearing for ADR:
- add explicit placement diagram (after control variant selection)
- define perf budget + overhead expectations
- define executor model (bounded queue, bounded concurrency, drop policy)
- define deterministic fingerprint input + bounded hashing
- define streaming semantics (v1: input mirrored, shadow output captured best-effort)
- clarify egress isolation (separate pool)
- add failure mode table w/ behavior and metrics

Ravi Menon: Yep. Also suggest we add a short section “Why not async replay from logs?” with the key point: replay requires retaining prompts longer + creates selection bias + not real-time for canary decisions.

Allison Grant: Please reference the data-model/cardinality ADR in the routing ADR (even just a link + one paragraph). Otherwise folks will invent dimensions and we’ll pay later.

Benji Okafor: I’ll paste the schema constraints into the ADR comments (max targets, fail-open only, header allowlist/denylist) so it stays consistent.

Priya Natarajan: Sounds good. Once those are incorporated I’m comfortable approving. I’ll re-review tomorrow.

Talia Benson: Thanks all. Ethan/Ravi/Mina can you take the ADR edits? Please tag @Priya Natarajan and me when the revised draft is ready, and we’ll move it to “Accepted” if there are no remaining blockers. :thumbsup:

Ethan Park: Acknowledged. Will push updates and ping.

title: #people-ops: 1767777777
preview:
people-ops-bot: :loudspeaker: Welcome announcement — Ash Sinha is joining Redwood Inference as Senior Manager, Platform Operations on 2024-06-03.
Maya Patel: awesome news! Ash will take ownership of Dedicated incident readiness, runbooks, and capacity handoffs.
Ash Sinha: thanks all  excited to join. happy to jump into the on-call rotation and review the current runbooks.
Diego Morales: @Ash Sinha can you do a sync Thu 10:00am PT to go over the Dedicated on-call rota and the customer capacity review on 2024-06-10?
Ash Sinha: Thu 10am PT works, and I'll join the 6/10 customer review.
Lizzy Mendoza: onboarding packet sent. @Ash Sinha please confirm pronouns and slack handle so I can update the org chart.
Ash Sinha: pronouns he/him, slack: @ash.sinha, timezone PDT.
Priya Natarajan: hiring note: we're opening the Platform Ops IC backfill today; scorecard and interview plan posted to #recruiting by EOD. referrals welcome.
Maya Patel: checklist for pre-start tasks:
- transfer Grafana dashboard ownership
- add Ash to Dedicated pager rota
- grant access to aws-prod-gamma and grafana-team
- invite to weekly capacity review and postmortem syncs
- assign initial runbook edits
Diego Morales: I'll take care of the calendar invites and add Ash to the 6/10 customer call.
Lizzy Mendoza: equipment ships 5/28, security training link in the packet, welcome lunch on 6/05.
Ash Sinha: appreciate the warm welcome everyone  looking forward to meeting the team and getting started.

title: #eng-runtime: 1766009999
preview:
Alex Chen: Morning async — quick status:
- Done: landed kernel changes for "bucketed" batching (grouping by prefix hash instead of strict arrival). Observed ~12% throughput uplift on short-sequence mix.
- Blocker: 95th p99 tail on >2k tokens increased by ~30ms for a small subset of canary traffic. Possibly KV eviction interplay.
- Today: triaging eviction + warmup timing, will run a longer perf sweep at 10:30 PT and push a canary with conservative eviction thresholds.

Priya Natarajan: Nice work on bucketed batching. Quick thought — are we miscounting batchable windows when slot steal occurs?
If so, batches might be smaller in the tail causing more context switches.

Alex Chen: good call. I saw some slot-steal logs that align. Will add extra metrics for 'batchable_window_hits' and 'slot_steals_during_batch'.

Mateo Ruiz: I can run the longer perf sweep on the dedicated pool if you want. I have a runbook snippet:
```bash
./scripts/run_sweep.sh --profile short_seq_heavy --duration 30m --collect=latency,kv_hits,batch_sizes
```
I'll kick it off once you push the canary.

Alex Chen: please do. I'll tag you when canary is live (~10:35).

Jin Park: quick ping — left a tiny review on the batching patch (inline comment about an atomic on the slot counter). Not blocking but could cause ABA if we don't CAS correctly.

Alex Chen: thanks, Jin. I'll address the CAS and re-run unit tests.

Sara (SRE): Heads up: if the canary triggers the tail increase we'll auto-rollout-fallback to the older kernel per the policy, but we want to avoid that. If you want more headroom, enable the 0.5s soft-sla in the canary group.

Alex Chen: good point. I'll set the canary soft-sla to 0.5s and increase evict grace from 60s -> 120s for the canary pool.

bench-bot: perf-sweep started @ 2026-03-17T17:35:12Z for profile short_seq_heavy (duration 30m). Collecting metrics: latency, kv_hits, batch_sizes.

Mateo Ruiz: started run on rtx-dedicated-7; will watch dashboards and post a short summary when done.

Priya Natarajan: small debug tip — when you instrument 'kv_eviction_reason', add values: LRU, TTL, MANUAL, BATCH_PRESSURE. That made analysis easier last time.

Alex Chen: will add those enums. Also adding a quick repro command for the tail locally:
```python
# repro snippet
from infra.tests import synthetic_client
cli = synthetic_client(profile='long_seq_spike')
cli.run(200, warmup=50, seq_len=2500)
```

Jin Park: if you need help reproducing I can pair after lunch. I have a short script that toggles warmup timing and shows kv warm hit rate per request.

Alex Chen: appreciate it. If the sweep shows stable p95/p99 I'll open a follow-up to relax the eviction tweak; if not, will revert and iterate.

Mateo Ruiz: bench summary (short): after 30m run — steady throughput +11%, p95 +18ms, p99 +42ms on the canary. KV warm-hit rate dropped ~3% during peak bursts. Posting full graphs to #eng-runtime-dash in 5.

Alex Chen: thanks Mateo. That aligns with slot-steal hypothesis. Next steps: add slot metrics, tweak eviction grace for canary, address Jin's CAS, re-run.

Priya Natarajan: if you want an eyeball on the metrics once posted ping me and I'll help interpret.

Sara (SRE): I'll watch auto-rollout during the next hour and standby to pause if we cross the rollback threshold.

Alex Chen: summary: triage slot-steal metrics + add eviction_reason enums + fix CAS + tweak canary evict grace -> re-sweep. Will post updates here. Thanks all. :thumbsup:

title: #people-ops: 1765432100
preview:
Amira Khan: Quick heads-up — we're standing up a small AI Trust & Safety org under Product to centralize policy, incident response, and customer escalations. Goal: consistent policy enforcement and faster cross-team response. No layoffs, just re-org.
Nora Elbaz: Recruiter note — I'll own the hire for "Head of AI Trust & Safety". Req goes live today. Target loop 4-6 weeks. Please DM referrals or drop them here.
Priya Natarajan: Nice. Who's the interim lead while we hire? And how do current policy owners get transitioned?
Samir Patel: I'll cover interim lead duties for ~8 weeks (policy routing, incident triage, escalation). Will keep my SRE slice minimal to avoid gaps.
Amira Khan: Thanks Samir. Short migration plan: current policy owners keep their reporting line for 30 days, then we'll move them into the new org in two waves. I'll post the timeline + org chart fragment here by EOD.
Lina Chavez: Recruiter ask — please post 3 outcome metrics and must-have vs nice-to-have for the role so I can tailor outreach.
Zoe Kim: I'll add the req to the hiring tracker and create a dedicated pipeline column "AI Trust". Also will schedule a quick sync for interviewers next week.
Priya Natarajan: Any immediate customer or SLA implications we need to flag to CS or Sales?
Amira Khan: No SLA changes. We'll notify CS only if we change account-level routing or if a customer-facing policy enforcement impacts a client. No external comms planned.
Nora Elbaz: Interview loop plan: 1) Recruiter screen, 2) Policy & ops case (take-home), 3) Cross-functional panel (Product, Infra, CS). Expect 3–5 week cadence if calendar aligns.
Samir Patel: I'll draft a 1-page incident triage runbook to hand off to the new hire. High-level flow: detection → triage → owner assign → mitigation → postmortem. Will add oncall contacts and escalation matrix.
Amira Khan: Quick FAQ to reduce DMs: 1) Compensation changes? No immediate changes. 2) Promotions/levels? Follow normal review cycles. 3) Concerns or questions? Reply here or DM People Ops.
Lina Chavez: Posting the JD in #jobs in ~20m. Recruiters will ping for referrals, please surface any internal candidates early.
Priya Natarajan: One request — please include a short list of expected cross-team collaborators (CS, Infra, Legal) in the job doc so interviewers can plan panels.
Nora Elbaz: Will do. Thanks everyone for the quick cover and help getting this off the ground. :sparkles:

title: #finance: 1763801234
preview:
Anna Chen: FYI — VelaHealth renewal invoice looks wrong. Platform fee prorated to 19 days and support billed as Standard, but contract has Enterprise support starting Feb 10. Attaching invoice snippet. @MikeGarcia @PriyaNatarajan can you take a look?

Mike Garcia: Can you paste the invoice lines? PDF is blocked on my phone.

Anna Chen: Sure — pasted below:
```
Invoice ID: INV-2026-0419
Customer: VelaHealth Inc.
Term start: 2026-03-01
Line items:
  - Platform Fee (private deployment): $10,800 (prorated, 19/30 days)
  - Reserved Capacity: $45,000
  - Support (Standard): $9,000
  - One-time migration credit: -$5,000
Total: $59,800
```

Priya Natarajan: This is the on-prem private license renewal, right? They upgraded to Enterprise support on 2026-02-10 per change order. Renewal date of Mar 1 should bill enterprise tier prorate from Feb10–Mar1, then full month thereafter. Looks like our billing applied Standard incorrectly.

Support Billing Bot: Invoice INV-2026-0419 (https://billing.redwood.ai/invoices/INV-2026-0419) generated by billing-pipeline v3.2.1 — attached PDF.

Jordan Lee: If this is an internal billing error we should issue a credit memo and reissue the renewal invoice. Legal prefers credits for clear mistakes rather than adjustments buried in next month. Keep a record.

Mike Garcia: Pulling contract 6543-PL now. Quick check — platform fee is a flat monthly $18,000 (no daily proration clause except at initial term). I suspect the migration credit (-$5,000) got reapplied at renewal, and the prorate logic in the pipeline used the migration start date instead of term start.

Anna Chen: Can you run numbers? Need estimate of the correction to tell the customer.

Mike Garcia: Quick math: they were invoiced platform $10,800 (19/30 * 18,000). They should be charged full $18,000 for the renewal. Delta = $7,200. For support: Enterprise monthly is $13,500; invoiced Standard $9,000 so underbilled $4,500 for the first month. Total shortfall = $11,700. If we also remove the migration credit that was incorrectly applied at renewal, that restores $5,000 back to invoice, but since migration credit should have been expired, we need to check its lifecycle. Net customer-facing credit to issue = $11,700 (we owe them that).

Priya Natarajan: Agreed. Customer asked procurement for a corrected invoice and explanation. I'd recommend issuing a credit memo for $11,700, reissuing the corrected renewal invoice (without the migration credit), and send an explanatory note.

Jordan Lee: Approved to issue credit memo. Use credit-memo template and include sentence: "Issued to correct billing error related to platform fee proration and support tier on renewal." No admission beyond that.

Anna Chen: I'll prepare the credit memo and updated invoice. Plan: (1) create credit memo $11,700, (2) reissue INV-2026-0419 as INV-2026-0419R with corrected lines, (3) notify customer and procurement with both docs. Assigning to @MikeGarcia to open a billing bug and to @Finance Ops to publish credit. ETA: EOD Friday.

Mike Garcia: :thumbsup: Opening internal bug now and pinging billing infra for a hotfix. I'll include tests so migration credits aren't reapplied at renewal. Ticket: BILL-9921.

Support Billing Bot: Ticket BILL-9921 created: "renewal proration + migration credit misapplied". Current status: triaged.

Finance Ops: Noted — will prepare credit memo template and queue for approval once Anna uploads the calc.

Anna Chen: Draft message to customer for review — please suggest wording:

"Hi <Procurement Contact>,
We identified an error on your recent renewal invoice INV-2026-0419 relating to platform fee proration and support tier application. We will issue a credit memo for $11,700 and reissue the corrected invoice within 3 business days. We apologize for the confusion and will expedite this for your procurement process. Please let me know if you need any additional documentation.
Best,
Anna Chen (Finance)"

Priya Natarajan: I'd add: "We appreciate your patience; procurement questions can be directed to myself or Anna." Also remove the word "error"? Customer prefers neutral phrasing sometimes. Maybe: "We identified an inconsistency on the invoice and have corrected it."

Anna Chen: Good call — changing wording now. Final draft: "We identified an inconsistency on your renewal invoice and have issued a credit memo for $11,700. A corrected invoice will be reissued within 3 business days. We apologize for the disruption and are available for any follow-up for procurement."

Mike Garcia: I'll follow up on BILL-9921 and provide ETA for fix.

Anna Chen: Thanks everyone. I'll close loop after credit issued and invoice reissued, then update the accounting records and revenue true-up.

Priya Natarajan: Thanks — looping in customer success to proactively message the customer afterwards.

Support Billing Bot: INV-2026-0419 status: awaiting credit memo.

Anna Chen: Closing update: created credit memo CM-2026-058, reissued invoice INV-2026-0419R, and notified VelaHealth this morning. Posting final docs to the portal. Will mark this thread resolved once finance posts the journal entries.

Finance Ops: Journal entries done, credit applied. Marking as resolved.

title: #people-ops: 1760501234
preview:
People Ops: Heads up everyone  We're making a small org change in Platform: the SDKs & DevEx team is becoming its own sub-team under Product. Ava Lin will join as Manager, Developer Experience (new hire) starting Mon May 12. She'll own SDKs, quickstarts, and developer onboarding flows.

Jordan Kim: welcome Ava! she'll shadow the SDK roadmap for 4 weeks and then take ownership of the public SDKs + internal templates. we'll run a 30/60 plan together.

Ava Lin: thrilled to join  immediate focus: unblock onboarding friction for private customers, and tidy SDK release cadence. happy to do quick intro calls with teams next week.

Priya Natarajan: congrats Ava  i'll coordinate on the compliance bits that touch SDKs (telemetry, cert pinning). note: i'll be 50% on Partner Security this quarter so i'll hand off routine SDK approvals.

Tomas Berg: recruiting will open a senior SDK engineer req to backfill handoff work. target interviews in 3 weeks. i'll post the req + screening rubric today.

Samir Patel: engineering ops here  Samir and Ava will run two pairing sessions next week to walk through the CI/CD for releases and the package signing flow. i'll add the slots to #eng-platform.

Nora Elbaz: customer success note: we'll update the onboarding playbook and include SDK troubleshooting doc. Ava, can you add a short FAQ for common developer pitfalls?

Ava Lin: yes — will draft a 1-pager FAQ and sample app template by end of week. also planning weekly office hours for the first month (Wed 9-10 PT).

People Ops: action list: 1) People Ops to update headcount & org chart by Thu EOD. 2) Recruiting (Tomas) to open senior SDK req. 3) Samir to post pairing slots and CI checklist. 4) Ava to post 30/60/90 and FAQ. 5) Nora to stash updated onboarding playbook.

Jordan Kim: scheduling a 30-min cross-functional intro Tues 11am PT (Product/Eng/SE/CS). optional but recommended.

Tomas Berg: req live in Greenhouse now, screening rubric in the drive.

Priya Natarajan: sharing the compliance checklist in the shared drive momentarily.

People Ops: thanks team  congrats Ava & welcome aboard! reactions: :tada: :wave: :rocket:

title: #people-ops: 1759991234
preview:
Amira Khan: Quick update 1 — we're shifting Product ev liaison for Runtime into a dedicated Lead role to tighten cross-team rollouts. This is a reorg, not a headcount change.
Jordan Kim: I'll pick up the interim lead for the Product ev liaison starting Monday for ~5 weeks while we hire. I'll continue to own the Dedicated roadmap in a reduced capacity.
Priya Natarajan: Thanks Jordan. Who covers your Platform PM meetings while you're interiming?
Jordan Kim: Mariam will cover the weekly Platform standups and OKR syncs. I'll hand over the current action items by EOD Fri.
Lina Chavez: Req goes live today: "Lead, Runtime Product Liaison". Recruiting lead is Lina. Please DM referrals or drop them here. Target loop 33 weeks.
Evan Carter: Can we get 3 outcome bullets for the JD (30/60/90)? Helps with screening.
Amira Khan: Sure: 30d: stable cross-team rollout cadence; 60d: reduce rollout incidents by 25%; 90d: operationalize one standardized rollout playbook. Posting this to the shared drive.
Lina Chavez: Thanks 1 Dropping the posting in #jobs in 10m. Recruiter ask: please list must-have vs nice-to-have in a reply so I can prioritize outreach.
Priya Natarajan: Any customer-facing risk during the interim?
Amira Khan: No immediate SLA changes. If we need to flip customer routing, we'll notify CS and affected accounts. Jordan will shadow oncall for two weeks during the handoff.
Jordan Kim: I'll publish a short transition doc today outlining which meetings I'm owning vs handing to Mariam and temporary escalation contacts. Scheduling a 30-min sync Tue 9:30 for cross-func alignment.
Lina Chavez: Interview plan: quick screen, ops case study, cross-func panel. Please comment preferred interviewers.
Evan Carter: Add Product lead + Runtime eng mgr to the panel. Also would be good to include a CS partner for customer impact questions.
Amira Khan: FAQ: 1) comp changes? No immediate changes. 2) promotions? handled via normal cycles. 3) questions? Reply here or DM People Ops. Thanks team for fast cover.

title: #people-ops: 1759012345
preview:
People Ops: Quick update for the org 1 1 1 Ava Lin is joining Redwood Inference on Mon May 5 as Senior Solutions Engineer, Private Deployments. She9ll focus on onboarding, pre-sales technical scoping, and ramping our on-prem reference architecture. Welcome Ava! :sparkles:

Ava Lin: hey all! excited to be here. quick notes: i'll be pairing with Priya for the first month on compliance handoffs, and with Samir on the onprem runbook. open to 1:1s next week 1 drop a calendar invite.

Priya Natarajan: welcome Ava 1 i'll send the compliance handoff packet (SOC2/KMS bits) and schedule a review. FYI i'm shifting 20% of my time to Partner Security through Q3, so we'll run shared ownership until the new SE is fully onboarded.

Jordan Kim: congrats Ava! Product will set up a 30-min sync so you can see roadmap items that touch VPC and private. also pinging Sales for any active deals that need technical handoff.

Nora Elbaz: looping in CS 1 I'll align customer-facing docs and update the playbook with an "SE onboarding checklist". Team, please flag any must-know escrow or compliance customers.

Samir Patel: operational note: Samir (eng) here 1 Ava and I will run 2 shadow deploys next week for the onprem installer end-to-end. putting the pairing slots in #eng-oncall. also opening a tiny RFC to capture installer edge cases.

People Ops: hiring/backfill note: we're converting one existing contractor seat into a full-time SE req to cover post-ramp coverage. Recruiting will open the req today; target hire by end of June. Tomas will own the loop.

Ava Lin: thanks all 1 a small ask: can someone share the last two customer private deployments and any lingering action items? that'll help prioritize my ramp.

Priya Natarajan: will share de-identified summaries in the Drive and add a short "regulatory checklist" for each.

Jordan Kim: scheduled the Product intro for Tue 11am PT, optional for PMs/SEs/Sales. link posted in #product.

People Ops: short action list: 1) Recruiting to open SE req; 2) Priya to share compliance packet; 3) Samir to post shadow deploy slots; 4) Ava to run intro office hours in week 1; 5) Nora to update SE playbook. We'll post the org update to the company directory once the req is live.

Nora Elbaz: congrats Ava 1 excited to work together. reactions: :tada: :coffee:

Jordan Kim: :thumbsup:

Samir Patel: adding first shadow slot now.

title: #people-ops: 1759004321
preview:
Amira Khan: Quick org note: Jordan is moving from Platform PM to run Dedicated Capacity as interim Head of Dedicated, starting Mon. No headcount changes, just a leadership shift to accelerate Dedicated roadmap :rocket:
Jordan Kim: Excited to take this on. I'll be 60% on Dedicated, 40% ramping down Platform projects. Mariam will take over the Platform PM cadence while I transition.
Mariam Okoye: Thanks Jordan. I'll pick up weekly stakeholder syncs and the mid-sprint demos. Can someone add me to the Platform OKR doc?
Diego Morales: I'll update the ownership matrix and add Mariam to the delivery channels. Also adding Jordan to the Dedicated oncall shadow rotation for the next 2 weeks.
Nora Elbaz: Hiring note: we'll open a req today for "Capacity Lead (Dedicated)" to be a permanent hire. Recruiting lead is Nora; target loop 46 weeks. Please DM referrals.
Priya Natarajan: Any immediate customer impacts or SLA changes?
Amira Khan: No SLA changes. We expect normal delivery cadence. If we need capacity fallbacks we'll route to Hosted or alternate Dedicated pools and notify affected accounts.
Jordan Kim: I'll publish a short transition doc with the responsibilities I'm keeping vs handing to Mariam and the new hire. Also scheduling a 30-min sync Tue 11am for product/infra/sales enablement.
Mariam Okoye: I'll take care of the Platform backlog for the next sprint. Posting a handoff checklist and the updated stakeholder list in #product by EOD.
Nora Elbaz: Recruiter checklist: please comment must-have vs nice-to-have and suggested interviewers. I'll post the job in #jobs in 15m.
Amira Khan: FAQ: 1) comp changes? No immediate changes. 2) promotion/levels? handled through normal cycles. 3) concerns? reply here or DM People Ops. Thanks everyone for quick coverage. :wave:

title: #people-ops: 1748501234
preview:
sara kim: Quick company update — short-term leadership rotation for Platform Ground. I'm stepping in as interim Platform Director for ~8 weeks starting Mon Apr 7 to cover Diego's parental leave. Will post calendar block later.

ajay patel: Thanks Sara — congrats Diego! quick Q: what are the top priorities I should own as runtime lead while you're covering director duties?

sara kim: Ajay — focus on the runtime P0s (KV cache regressions, nightly OOMs), keep the canary rollout for v2.3 paused until we have the perf traces cleared. I'll own cross-team escalations and external stakeholders.

ana torres: I'll take capacity & infra ops — planning to hold a 30m handoff with Diego + oncall tonight. Can someone drop the handoff checklist here? :white_check_mark:

people-ops-bot: Reminder: submit interim leadership coverage form — https://intranet.redwood/people-ops/interim-cover (required for payroll/approvals).

priya naranjan: tagging @people-ops — do we need a change to headcount or is this a temporary IC backfill? Also, what's the suggested band for the 4-6mo backfill?

people-ops-bot: People Ops note: short-term backfills can be engaged as contractors or temp FTEs. Please complete the intake form above; recruiting will advise banding based on scope.

david c.: I'll own day-to-day oncall routing and triage. Ajay — can you own the weekly runtime sync doc so I can escalate when needed?

ajay patel: Yep, I'll own the runtime weekly doc. Putting a stub in the docs repo now.

sara kim: Hand-off doc (work in progress): https://docs.redwood/platform-ground/handoffs/2026-interim-sara-v1 — contains list of active rolling upgrades, canary status, and current bugs. Ana — please add infra runbook links.

ana torres: Adding runbooks now. Also, proposing a short org chart update for the console so cross-functional PMs know who to contact.

priya naranjan: Cool. Once the intake form is in, I'll coordinate with recruiting and share a hiring timeline (target to have contractor in place within 3 weeks). FYI — approval usually ~5 business days once form is submitted.

sara kim: Thank you all. Plan: 1) submit interim coverage form today (people-ops) 2) recruiting opens backfill req tomorrow 3) 1:1 handoffs between Diego and each interim owner this week. I'll post weekly check-in cadence. Any blockers call them out here. :heart:

david c.: no blockers from infra. Thanks — we'll sync after the handoff call.

ajay patel: One last note — pausing non-critical infra changes from Apr 7–21 to reduce blast radius during the rotation. Will post the freeze window in #eng-alerts.

sara kim: Good call. I'll add that to the calendar invite and notify PMs.

ana torres: Thanks team — glad we have a clear plan. :party_parrot:

title: #eng: 1747333303
preview:
Priya Natarajan: We’re close to flipping the oncall handoff for the PLG signal router. Can folks drop the *must-have* metrics + alert conditions? Thinking HubSpot 429s, retries, dropped events, lag, etc. Also owners + dashboard link placeholders so we can wire this into PagerDuty.

Logan Wright: +1. From telemetry/analytics side, the top things that will bite us:
- event lag (telemetry -> analytics table -> scoring output)
- aggregator job failures / partial writes
- dropped events / DLQ growth
If we don’t alert on lag, sales will just see “nothing happening” and assume routing is broken.

Mei Lin: For the daily aggregation job (ENG-7732 / PR-28458), we should alert on:
- `aggregation_job_success` (1/0) per run
- `aggregation_rows_written` (unexpectedly low vs trailing 7d baseline)
- `aggregation_backfill_runs` (if backfill is running, suppress some lag alerts)
Also a gauge for `analytics_table_partition_freshness_hours`.

Caleb Johnson: Ingestion/data quality:
- `telemetry_event_ingest_lag_seconds` p95 and max
- `telemetry_events_dropped_total` (with reason label: schema_invalid, auth, throttled)
- `telemetry_events_dlq_depth`
We should page only on sustained DLQ growth + lag, not on occasional schema_invalid spikes (docs instrumentation can be noisy).

Ethan Park: HubSpot integration metrics (PR-28503 + PR-28544):
- `hubspot_requests_total` by endpoint
- `hubspot_429s_total` + `hubspot_rate_limited_seconds_total`
- `hubspot_5xx_total`
- `hubspot_retries_total` + `hubspot_retry_queue_depth`
- `hubspot_task_create_failures_total`
- `hubspot_company_upsert_failures_total`
Also *idempotency* issues are subtle — a counter for `hubspot_deduped_updates_total` would help validate we’re not spamming timeline events.

Nadia Rahman: Scoring service should expose:
- `signals_created_total` (by signal_type)
- `signals_suppressed_total` (by suppression_reason)
- `signals_routed_total` (by destination=hubspot)
- `signals_failed_total` (validation_failed, missing_company_mapping, hubspot_error)
And a histogram: `signal_end_to_end_latency_seconds` (telemetry timestamp -> task created)

Marcus Lin: From security side: please add a metric + alert if we ever attempt to send disallowed fields to HubSpot.
Like `hubspot_payload_policy_violations_total` (should be 0 always). If >0, page the oncall + auto-disable routing.

Talia Benson: Capturing this for the runbook. Can we agree on *alert thresholds* (page vs ticket)? Proposed:
P0 page:
- HubSpot 429s sustained (>= 10% of requests for 10m) OR retry queue depth continuously increasing for 15m
- End-to-end lag p95 > 6h (excluding backfills)
- Task create failures > 2% for 10m
P1 ticket:
- 5xx spikes but recovering
- row writes drop >50% vs 7d median
Thoughts?

Logan Wright: 6h lag feels high given we’re doing daily windows, but the expectation from sales is “I should see tasks same day.” I’d set:
- ticket at p95 > 2h for 2 consecutive hours
- page at p95 > 6h for 2 consecutive hours
and explicitly suppress during backfill.

Mei Lin: +1 to suppression during backfills. We can tag runs with `window_type=daily|backfill` and key alerts off daily only.
Also add:
- `aggregation_window_complete_timestamp` gauge (epoch) so we can compute freshness in Grafana easily.

Ethan Park: On 429s: HubSpot rate limits are bursty. I’d rather page on “routing stopped” symptoms:
- `hubspot_task_create_success_rate` < 95% for 15m (page)
- `hubspot_retry_queue_depth` > 5k and increasing (page)
And a warning when 429 ratio > 5% for 10m (ticket) so we tune batching.

Caleb Johnson: For dropped events: page if `telemetry_events_dropped_total{reason!=schema_invalid}` increases by > 1k in 10m AND lag p95 > 1h. Otherwise we’ll page every time a client SDK rolls.

Priya Natarajan: This is great. Let’s lock a minimal set for day-1 oncall:
1) HubSpot delivery health (success rate, retries, 429/5xx)
2) E2E lag
3) DLQ depth / dropped events
4) Job/run success
Anything beyond that can be phase 2.

Nadia Rahman: Please also include a “volume sanity” panel:
- `signals_created_total` by type
- `signals_routed_total` by region
If we suddenly double our routing volume, that’s usually a threshold regression.

Talia Benson: Owners?
- HubSpot metrics/alerts: @Ethan Park
- Ingestion lag/DLQ/drops: @Caleb Johnson
- Aggregation freshness + writes: @Mei Lin
- Overall dashboards + PD integration: I can coordinate but need a DRI for the service

Priya Natarajan: DRI for service ops is Logan (Telemetry & Analytics) for this pilot. Ethan owns HubSpot path. I’ll be escalation for platform.

DeployBot: reminder: PR-28630 (metrics hooks) merged to main. Staging deploy scheduled 18:00 PT.

Logan Wright: Dashboard placeholders (will fill once Grafana panels are live):
- Signal Router Overview: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/PLG_SIGNAL_ROUTER/overview (TBD)
- HubSpot Delivery: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/PLG_SIGNAL_ROUTER/hubspot (TBD)
- Ingestion + DLQ: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/PLG_SIGNAL_ROUTER/ingestion (TBD)
Runbook placeholder: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/ENG/PLG+Signal+Router+Oncall+Runbook (TBD)

Marcus Lin: +1 on runbook including “kill switch” steps if payload policy violations fire.

Priya Natarajan: Action items:
- Ethan: define alert rules for HubSpot success rate + retry queue depth (and add to PD service)
- Mei/Caleb: freshness + DLQ alerts with backfill suppression
- Talia: create runbook skeleton + link to INT-1843 + suppression guidance
Let’s aim to have alerts in place before we expand pilot cohort next week.

Nadia Rahman: Please also include a “volume sanity” panel:
- `signals_created_total` by type
- `signals_routed_total` by region
If we suddenly double our routing volume, that’s usually a threshold regression.

Talia Benson: Owners?
- HubSpot metrics/alerts: @Ethan Park
- Ingestion lag/DLQ/drops: @Caleb Johnson
- Aggregation freshness + writes: @Mei Lin
- Overall dashboards + PD integration: I can coordinate but need a DRI for the service

Priya Natarajan: DRI for service ops is Logan (Telemetry & Analytics) for this pilot. Ethan owns HubSpot path. I’ll be escalation for platform.

DeployBot: reminder: PR-28630 (metrics hooks) merged to main. Staging deploy scheduled 18:00 PT.

Logan Wright: Dashboard placeholders (will fill once Grafana panels are live):
- Signal Router Overview: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/PLG_SIGNAL_ROUTER/overview (TBD)
- HubSpot Delivery: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/PLG_SIGNAL_ROUTER/hubspot (TBD)
- Ingestion + DLQ: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/PLG_SIGNAL_ROUTER/ingestion (TBD)
Runbook placeholder: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/ENG/PLG+Signal+Router+Oncall+Runbook (TBD)

Marcus Lin: +1 on runbook including “kill switch” steps if payload policy violations fire.

Priya Natarajan: Action items:
- Ethan: define alert rules for HubSpot success rate + retry queue depth (and add to PD service)
- Mei/Caleb: freshness + DLQ alerts with backfill suppression
- Talia: create runbook skeleton + link to INT-1843 + suppression guidance
Let’s aim to have alerts in place before we expand pilot cohort next week.

title: #eng-platform: 1747160501
preview:
Ethan Park: starting a thread on HubSpot auth for the signal router service — OAuth scopes, token rotation, secret storage, audit trail requirements. we need to lock this before we merge the integration PRs.

David Kim: :wave: from HubSpot side: we should use a HubSpot public app w/ OAuth (not a private app token). private app tokens are easy but auditing + rotation is worse and security is going to hate it.

Marcus Lin: +1. private app tokens are basically long-lived bearer secrets. OAuth w/ refresh tokens is the right default. also want least-privilege scopes.

Ethan Park: ok. current plan in PR uses OAuth refresh token stored in Secrets Manager, access token cached in memory w/ TTL. question is: which scopes are actually required for MVP? we’re doing:
- company lookup by domain / name
- upsert company properties (custom properties)
- create timeline note/activity
- create task assigned to owner

Tyler Benson: from marketing ops/workflows: we’ll also have workflows that read the properties + list membership, but the service itself shouldn’t need workflow scopes.

Isabelle Laurent: sales ops angle: we need ownership assignment to respect existing owner + round robin fallback. does that require any extra scopes to set owner id? and do we need to read owners?

David Kim: updating owner on a company uses `crm.objects.companies.write` and setting `hubspot_owner_id` property. reading owners list is separate (`crm.objects.owners.read`). if you want to map email -> ownerId deterministically you’ll need that. alternative: keep mapping in our side (config) and avoid the owners API.

Logan Wright: would prefer config mapping for pilot (NA only). keep CRM calls minimal to avoid 429s. owner mapping can be a static table keyed by region/team.

Ethan Park: makes sense. but we still need to preserve existing owner. that means we need to read company properties including owner id.

David Kim: yup, `crm.objects.companies.read`.

Marcus Lin: for tasks + notes: tasks are engagement objects. scope is `crm.objects.tasks.write` + `crm.objects.tasks.read` if we ever query them. notes are `crm.objects.notes.write`. there’s also `crm.objects.contacts.*` if we tie to contacts, but MVP is company-level right?

Ethan Park: MVP is company-level. we attach note + task to company record.

David Kim: attaching associations might require `crm.schemas.*`? no, just using standard associations should be fine. but you may need `crm.objects.*.write` for the object types you create.

Caleb Johnson: do we need any scopes for “timeline events”? hubspot has that “timeline” API but I think we’re just using notes.

David Kim: correct. don’t use Timeline Extensions for MVP. notes are enough and show up in the record timeline.

Priya Natarajan: can we write down a minimal scope set and get security signoff? I want this stable so we don’t have to redo the app later.

Ethan Park: draft minimal scopes:
```
crm.objects.companies.read
crm.objects.companies.write
crm.objects.notes.write
crm.objects.tasks.write
oauth
```
optional:
```
crm.objects.owners.read
```

Marcus Lin: question: does HubSpot require the `oauth` scope explicitly? usually it’s implied but leaving it in docs is fine.

David Kim: in HubSpot app config, you select “scopes” and OAuth is implied. but in our internal docs we should list “OAuth app w/ refresh token flow” as requirement.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: looping in. couple requirements from security:
1) refresh token must be stored in a managed secrets store (AWS Secrets Manager ok; Vault ok). encrypted at rest + restricted IAM.
2) no tokens in logs. redact + structured logging.
3) token rotation policy: refresh token rotation at least every 90 days OR on employee/offboarding / suspected compromise. confirm hubspot refresh tokens can be revoked + reissued w/o downtime.
4) audit trail: record every token exchange + every API call outcome? at minimum: who/what created/updated CRM objects (service identity), correlation IDs, and an immutable log trail (CloudWatch + retention).

Ethan Park: on (3) — we can revoke the app install and re-install to re-issue refresh token. but that’s disruptive. is there a way to rotate refresh token without uninstall?

David Kim: HubSpot issues new refresh token on re-auth. you can run the OAuth flow again and swap the token. not as disruptive as uninstall; but still “manual” unless we build a token rotation job.

Marcus Lin: so implement “rotate by re-auth” playbook. store token versioned, allow dual token overlap during swap? easiest: write new secret value, deploy picks it up, old one invalidated later.

Priya Natarajan: we should design the secret fetch to support hot reload (polling) or at least quick restart.

Ethan Park: PR currently loads secret at startup. we can add periodic refresh (e.g., every 10m) to pick up secret changes + avoid redeploy.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: periodic refresh is good, but be careful: if HubSpot rate limits token exchange, don’t re-auth too frequently. only refresh access tokens as needed, not refresh tokens.

Caleb Johnson: also note: we saw HubSpot 429s in other contexts. token refresh should be resilient — exponential backoff + jitter, and queue writes.

Logan Wright: +1. plus add metrics: `hubspot_token_refresh_success`, `hubspot_token_refresh_fail`, and `hubspot_auth_401` count. auth 401 spikes are the canary for token rotation issues.

Tyler Benson: re: audit trail — ops needs to be able to answer “why did this account get outreach.” can we include signal id in note body + task subject so it’s traceable?

Ethan Park: yes. note template includes `plg_signal_id`, `signal_type`, `window_start/end`, and `router_version`.

Isabelle Laurent: and please include “source = product signal router” so reps don’t think it’s marketing automation. also if the service updates owner, log it.

Marcus Lin: secrets storage: prefer AWS Secrets Manager with KMS CMK, restricted to service role, no human read. if humans need rotation, use a break-glass role with approval.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: agree. also store HubSpot app client secret in same secret bundle. don’t keep it in CI vars.

David Kim: HubSpot app creds: client_id, client_secret. refresh token is per portal install. we’ll have one portal (Redwood’s) for now. if we add sandbox portal later, we’ll need separate install + separate refresh token.

Ethan Park: ok, so secret structure (conceptually):
- HUBSPOT_CLIENT_ID
- HUBSPOT_CLIENT_SECRET
- HUBSPOT_REFRESH_TOKEN
- HUBSPOT_PORTAL_ID (optional)

Marcus Lin: don’t store as env vars in plaintext. load them at runtime.

Priya Natarajan: what about “who did the API call” visibility in HubSpot? will it show as “App: Redwood Signal Router”?

David Kim: yes, changes will be attributed to the app (good). tasks/notes will show created by integration.

SecurityBot: Reminder: Security review checklist for third-party SaaS integrations: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/security/saas-integrations-checklist (Token storage, logging, rotation, vendor scopes, incident response)

Ethan Park: action items:
1) Ethan: update ENG PR to (a) minimize scopes to list above, (b) add secret hot-reload (poll), (c) add auth-related metrics.
2) David: confirm exact scopes required for notes+tasks association to company (any hidden gotchas) + share HubSpot app config screenshot.
3) Marcus/Aisha: sign off on 90-day refresh token rotation runbook + break-glass access.
4) Tyler/Isabelle: confirm we don’t need contact scopes for MVP + owner mapping approach.

Tyler Benson: marketing ops ok with company-only for MVP. we can add contacts later for lifecycle emails.

Isabelle Laurent: sales ops ok with config mapping for NA pilot. we’ll revisit owner API if we expand teams/regions.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: once you push the changes, tag me + Marcus. include a short “data in/out” section in the ADR: what’s stored, what’s logged, retention.

Marcus Lin: also add a log scrubber test (unit test) to ensure no token strings appear. we’ve been burned by this before.

Ethan Park: :thumbsup: will do. will post PR link in this thread when ready.

David Kim: fyi HubSpot OAuth scopes naming sometimes differs in UI vs API docs. I’ll send the canonical list.

Logan Wright: last ask: make sure we set a reasonable access token refresh buffer (refresh when <60s remaining) to avoid concurrent refresh stampede under load.

Ethan Park: yep. will use a singleflight lock around refresh.

Priya Natarajan: thanks all — keep this tight, we want minimal risk before pilot rollout.

title: #partnerships: 1746901202
preview:
Camila Reyes: pulling together options for reverse ETL / enrichment to get PLG signals + account context into HubSpot without us building a million one-off syncs. What vendors should we evaluate + do we even want a partner here vs build in-house?

Ben Carter: +1. Context: MVP is telemetry -> analytics -> scoring svc -> HubSpot API. Works, but we keep getting asks for “enrich with firmographic / intent / billing tier / workspace rollups” and it’s turning into a mini-ETL project.

Tyler Benson: from Marketing Ops POV: reverse ETL vendor would help us keep HubSpot properties sane + manage workflows/lists. But I’m nervous about another system writing to HubSpot without clear ownership + suppression logic.

David Kim: fwiw HubSpot API limits are real (already seeing 429s). If a vendor blasts updates, we’ll be back in rate-limit hell unless we design around it.

Isabelle Laurent: also need deterministic ownership rules. If vendor updates company fields it can accidentally trigger workflow reassignment. :grimacing:

Camila Reyes: vendors I’m hearing in the wild: Hightouch, Census, RudderStack (Reverse ETL), Segment (profiles), Tray/Workato (iPaaS-ish). For enrichment: Clearbit, Apollo, ZoomInfo, People Data Labs, 6sense (but $$$ / heavy).

Ben Carter: what’s the actual gap we’re solving?
1) reverse ETL: warehouse -> HubSpot property updates reliably (idempotent, rate-limited, retries, audit logs)
2) enrichment: append company size/industry + maybe domains + region
3) “intent” enrichment: web visits / doc views / etc (we already have docs instrumentation via @Elliot Price)

Logan Wright: engineering angle: we already have a scoring service; adding a queue + batcher for HubSpot writes is not crazy. Reverse ETL vendor mainly helps for *ongoing* property sync + UI for ops, plus connectors to enrichment sources.

Priya Natarajan: agreed. If we can keep the write-path controlled (our router service), I’d rather not introduce a second writer unless we can lock it down.

Tyler Benson: if we use a vendor, can we constrain them to only write to a limited set of properties? e.g. “enrichment_*” properties and leave “signal_*” + ownership fields to our service.

David Kim: yes, but HubSpot permissions are coarse. We can do it via conventions + workflow guardrails, not true per-property auth. We can also use separate private app tokens/accounts, but it’s still same portal.

Isabelle Laurent: +1 to limiting blast radius. Also any vendor needs to respect our existing owner preservation rules (don’t auto-enroll companies that already have an AE/CSM owner).

Camila Reyes: ok so sounds like two tracks:
A) reverse ETL vendor for enrichment-only fields + lists
B) keep signal routing + task creation in-house

Ben Carter: that seems right. Then we can evaluate vendors on:
- rate limit handling / batch semantics
- dedupe + idempotency (we do upserts by domain/org_id)
- audit trail + replay
- security posture (SOC2, data retention)
- ability to run on limited properties

Dr. Aisha Rahman: jumping in w/ security lens: any vendor touching customer usage-derived data in a 3rd party needs a clear DPA + documented retention + access controls. For PLG signals, we’re trying to be privacy-minimal. Prefer: vendor only gets company identifiers + firmographics, not raw usage.

Ben Carter: good call. We can keep raw usage + signal scoring internal, and only sync “signal summary” fields into HubSpot.

Elliot Price: for intent, we already have docs events piped into the analytics dataset. I’d rather not add a 3rd party tracker that creates a separate intent source of truth.

Camila Reyes: re vendors, I already have a warm intro to Hightouch (they’re pitching “reverse ETL + enrichment connectors”). Census is similar. RudderStack is more event pipeline + reverse ETL but could be heavier.

Tyler Benson: Hightouch/Census both have pretty strong HubSpot connectors + can manage computed traits in warehouse. I like that it’s easier for ops to tweak without eng deploys.

Logan Wright: but if they compute traits, we need to define the canonical logic. Otherwise we’ll have “meeting_booked_last_30d” defined three different ways.

David Kim: also please no one turns on “two-way sync” by accident. :sweat_smile:

Isabelle Laurent: +1. We should write down “single source of truth” rules:
- signal scoring + qualification: internal service
- enrichment fields: vendor
- ownership + task creation: internal service + HubSpot workflows

Camila Reyes: does anyone have strong preference between Hightouch vs Census?

Ben Carter: no strong preference. Hightouch has more partnerships momentum + I saw they have better rate-limit knobs. Census tends to be a bit simpler. We should run 2 short eval calls.

Tyler Benson: agree. Also ask both about:
- sandbox/test HubSpot portal support
- change management (preview diffs, dry-run)
- alerting when sync fails

Dr. Aisha Rahman: add: where do they store data? can they do EU data residency? and can we configure retention to <30d for any identifiers.

Priya Natarajan: and how they handle retries/backoff for 429s specifically (HubSpot has annoying rolling windows).

Camila Reyes: action items / next steps:
1) I’ll schedule vendor intros: Hightouch + Census (30 min each). Target next week.
2) @David Kim can you share our current HubSpot API rate limits + what endpoints we’re hitting most? (so we can ask pointed questions)
3) @Tyler Benson + @Isabelle Laurent can you list the exact HubSpot properties/workflows that are “hands off” / sensitive (ownership, lifecycle stage, etc.)
4) @Dr. Aisha Rahman can we get a quick checklist for vendor security review (DPA, SOC2, retention, sub-processors)?

David Kim: yep. I’ll drop notes here later today + a link to the internal rate-limit incident ticket.

Tyler Benson: I’ll put together a “do not touch” list + the properties we’d *want* a vendor to populate (industry, employee band, HQ region, technographics maybe).

Isabelle Laurent: I’ll add the routing/ownership gotchas + which workflows can accidentally reassign.

Ben Carter: also, can we define what “success” is for a vendor?
- reduce eng time spent on enrichment by X hrs/week
- keep signal task creation success rate >99%
- no increase in complaint rate (unwanted outreach) due to bad enrichment

Camila Reyes: :thumbsup: will add to the eval doc.

Camila Reyes: scheduling: proposing Tue 11am PT for Hightouch, Thu 10am PT for Census. Anyone want to join live? (partnerships + ops + security would be good)

Dr. Aisha Rahman: I can do Thu.

David Kim: Tue works.

Tyler Benson: both work.

Ben Carter: I’ll join Tue.

Camila Reyes: cool I’ll send cal invites + add agenda/questions in the invite.

David Kim: (reaction) :white_check_mark:

Priya Natarajan: if the vendor route looks messy, we should be ready with a “build it ourselves” fallback: batcher + queue + suppression rules + minimal enrichment via our own lookup (domain -> region).

Camila Reyes: agreed. We’ll treat vendor as phase 2, not a blocker for signal routing MVP.

- alerting when sync fails

Dr. Aisha Rahman: add: where do they store data? can they do EU data residency? and can we configure retention to <30d for any identifiers.

Priya Natarajan: and how they handle retries/backoff for 429s specifically (HubSpot has annoying rolling windows).

Camila Reyes: action items / next steps:
1) I’ll schedule vendor intros: Hightouch + Census (30 min each). Target next week.
2) @David Kim can you share our current HubSpot API rate limits + what endpoints we’re hitting most? (so we can ask pointed questions)
3) @Tyler Benson + @Isabelle Laurent can you list the exact HubSpot properties/workflows that are “hands off” / sensitive (ownership, lifecycle stage, etc.)
4) @Dr. Aisha Rahman can we get a quick checklist for vendor security review (DPA, SOC2, retention, sub-processors)?

David Kim: yep. I’ll drop notes here later today + a link to the internal rate-limit incident ticket.

Tyler Benson: I’ll put together a “do not touch” list + the properties we’d *want* a vendor to populate (industry, employee band, HQ region, technographics maybe).

Isabelle Laurent: I’ll add the routing/ownership gotchas + which workflows can accidentally reassign.

Ben Carter: also, can we define what “success” is for a vendor?
- reduce eng time spent on enrichment by X hrs/week
- keep signal task creation success rate >99%
- no increase in complaint rate (unwanted outreach) due to bad enrichment

Camila Reyes: :thumbsup: will add to the eval doc.

Camila Reyes: scheduling: proposing Tue 11am PT for Hightouch, Thu 10am PT for Census. Anyone want to join live? (partnerships + ops + security would be good)

Dr. Aisha Rahman: I can do Thu.

David Kim: Tue works.

Tyler Benson: both work.

Ben Carter: I’ll join Tue.

Camila Reyes: cool I’ll send cal invites + add agenda/questions in the invite.

David Kim: (reaction) :white_check_mark:

Priya Natarajan: if the vendor route looks messy, we should be ready with a “build it ourselves” fallback: batcher + queue + suppression rules + minimal enrichment via our own lookup (domain -> region).

Camila Reyes: agreed. We’ll treat vendor as phase 2, not a blocker for signal routing MVP.

title: #incidents: 1745554322
preview:
IncidentBot: \u26a0\ufe0f ALERT: Elevated 504/5xx rates observed for Crescent Bank (account_id: cb-402) in us-west-2 on /v1/chat.generate. Triggered 2024-08-01T14:12:02Z
Mira Li: oncall SRE picking up. seeing sustained queue backlog in the serving frontend; p95 latency spiked to 2.1s. Routing shows traffic stuck on pool redwood-dedicated-usw2-2.
Tom Anders: I can see autoscaler failed to add nodes; `autoscaler-controller` logs show `rate limit exceeded` talking to cloud provider quota API. Also some workers stuck in STATUS=RESTARTING.
Priya Natarajan: taking pager. First action: confirm if dedicated pool quota or control-plane throttling. `rdwctl pools describe redwood-dedicated-usw2-2` and `kubectl get pods -n inference -o wide --selector=pool=redwood-dedicated-usw2-2`
Carlos Ruiz: running those now. Output: `pool desired=12 running=8 pending=4`, pending stuck due to provider quota error: `APILimitExceeded: Request rate too high`. Two pods with crashloop backoff referencing `failed to mount nvme-shared-cache`.
Mira Li: that explains sticky scatters — some requests are timing out waiting for cached shard mounts. Can we enable fallback to shared cached images and route new sessions to redwood-dedicated-usw2-1?
Tom Anders: yes, route change doable: `rdwctl route set --pool redwood-dedicated-usw2-1 --weight 1.0 --region us-west-2`. But need to ensure it has capacity for enterprise in-flight sessions.
Priya Natarajan: did the route change. traffic now shifting. 5xx down from 12% -> 3.2% in last 3m. Still seeing intermittent 504s for longer sessions.
Lydia (AM): cc: Account team. Crescent Bank contacts: primary ops@crescentbank.com (Megan Torres), secondary infra@crescentbank.com (Devon Lee), legal@crescentbank.com. Their contractual SLA: 99.9% monthly for dedicated pools with 4-hour response SLA for P1s.
Mira Li: drafting customer-specific impact note. Key points: which endpoints affected, recommended immediate mitigation (use lighter model or retries), ETA for next update. Avoid guessing RCA.
Priya Natarajan: root cause likely twofold: provider rate-limits prevented autoscaler from provisioning replacement nodes + stale cache mounts causing worker crashloops. Both together caused capacity shortfall for the dedicated pool.
Carlos Ruiz: repro steps for nodes: failed to mount because host kernel timed out on NVMe hotplug after orchestration patch last night. I'll open a separate issue with eng-infra. For immediate fix, I reprovisioned one node from golden image which has stable NVMe mounting behavior.
Mira Li: customer note draft (internal):
```
Crescent Bank Ops team,
We're investigating elevated 5xx/504 responses impacting your /v1/chat.generate traffic in us-west-2. Symptoms: higher latency and some requests returning 504. Mitigation in progress: we've shifted traffic to an alternate dedicated pool and reprovisioned affected nodes. Recommended client mitigations: enable short retries with jitter (max 3 attempts) and consider lowering request concurrency for long sessions. We will provide updates every 10 minutes until resolved. No customer data was accessed.
Regards,
Redwood Inference Ops
```
Mira Li: adding tailored impact statement: "Impact limited to dedicated pool redwood-dedicated-usw2-2; if you have failover to our other dedicated pool or hosted region enabled, traffic should succeed there. If you rely solely on redwood-dedicated-usw2-2, you may experience service degradation until reprovisioning completes."
Lydia (AM): please include explicit contact routing: sending to Megan Torres (ops), cc Devon Lee (infra); escalation to legal only if outage exceeds 60m. I will send the note and loop in AM shadow.
PagerDuty: Incident acknowledged by Priya Natarajan. Priority P1 for impacted enterprise customer.
Tom Anders: reprovisioning notes: kicked off node reprovision for 3 nodes, and temporarily increased provider API backoff window to avoid further quota hits. Also toggled `mount_retry=5` on agent to help NVMe binds.
Carlos Ruiz: after reprovision one node returned healthy, 504s dropped. But there are still two pods in CrashLoopBackOff due to corrupted cache metadata; I'm forcing a cache rehydrate from S3 for those workers: `rdwctl cache rehydrate --worker worker-23`
Priya Natarajan: sending internal status update template for comms channel (ops-team/incidents):
```
[INCIDENT] P1 - Crescent Bank - us-west-2 - ongoing
Impact: Elevated 5xx/504 for /v1/chat.generate for single enterprise dedicated pool
Mitigation: traffic routed to redwood-dedicated-usw2-1; reprovisioning nodes; cache rehydrate in progress
Customer contacts: Megan Torres (ops@crescentbank.com), Devon Lee (infra@crescentbank.com)
Next update: +10m
Owner: Mira Li (SRE)
```
Mira Li: Nora, please post private status page entry and schedule a public advisory if other customers are impacted.
Nora (status): drafting private post: "Crescent Bank - partial outage in us-west-2 resolved? pending confirmation" and will publish enterprise-visible update.
Lydia (AM): sent the tailored customer note and AMs report back that Crescent Bank has acknowledged and switched a subset of traffic to their backup region. They've asked for ETA on full restoration. Do not promise less than 20 minutes.
Priya Natarajan: verifying workers: `rdwctl workers list --pool redwood-dedicated-usw2-2` now shows running=11 desired=12 pending=1. Error rate at customer endpoint back to 0.9% and latency p95 420ms.
Carlos Ruiz: cache rehydrate completed for worker-23 and 24. Crash loops cleared. We've also added a transient guard to the autoscaler to slow provisioning attempts when provider returns 429s. Patch queued: autoscaler/backoff-throttle-20240801.
Tom Anders: filing follow-up tickets: INFRA-987: audit kernel hotplug patch; AUTOSCALE-112: provider quota handling; CACHE-561: rehydrate safeties.
Mira Li: OK. Drafting resolution/update for the customer: include timeline, RCA (provider rate limit + NVMe mount timeouts after orchestration change), mitigation steps we took, recommended client-side recommendations, and action items we will track with estimated timelines.
Mira Li: customer resolution draft:
```
Crescent Bank team,
Update: We have restored normal service for your us-west-2 traffic. Timeline: incident started ~14:11 UTC; traffic shifted to alternate pool and reprovisioning/cache rehydrate completed by 14:42 UTC. Root cause: a combination of provider API rate limiting which prevented timely autoscaler provisioning, and NVMe host mount timeouts caused by a recent orchestration hotplug change. Mitigations: routed traffic to redwood-dedicated-usw2-1, reprovisioned affected nodes, rehydrated local cache stores, and added autoscaler backoff throttling to avoid provider quota exhaustion. Recommended client actions: enable retries with jitter (max 3), consider multi-region failover for critical flows. We will follow up with a full postmortem and action item timeline.
Apologies for any disruption.
4cc Redwood Inference Ops
```
Lydia (AM): sent. Customer reply: "Thanks for the rapid response. Please share postmortem and let us know if we should change our failover config."
PagerDuty: Incident resolved by Priya Natarajan. Severity lowered to P2; auto-close pending postmortem.
Mira Li: creating incident ticket RDW-INC-2024-0801-cb402. Action items: 1) infra to assess orchestration hotplug patch impact (ETA 2 days); 2) autoscaler provider-backoff improvements (ETA 1 week); 3) add customer-specific autoscaler canary flag for dedicated pools (ETA 3 weeks).
Carlos Ruiz: noted. Uploading forensic logs and cache snapshots to s3://redwood-forensics/cb-402/20240801/ for RCA.
Nora (status): private post to Crescent Bank account page published; public status page shows brief advisory: "Transient service degradation in us-west-2 affecting a small group of dedicated-pool customers; resolved."
Mira Li: handing off postmortem ownership to Tom Anders for RCA write-up. Tom, please include timeline, root cause, and the tailored customer communications we used.
Tom Anders: acknowledged. I'll take the RCA and the Jira ticket. Closing live incident thread. :white_check_mark:

title: #people-ops: 1742509876
preview:
Maya (People Ops): Quick org update — Priya Natarajan will join Redwood Inference as Director, Runtime Reliability, reporting to Tom. Start date: May 5. This creates two immediate needs: (1) a backfill for a Senior Reliability Engineer (L4) and (2) an interim lead for the observability stack for ~6 weeks while we onboard. We want an internal-first hiring window; details below.

Tom (CTO): Huge yay — Priya's background on low-latency inference infra is exactly what we need. We'll allocate a small contract budget to cover the ramp. :tada:

Priya Natarajan: Hi everyone! Really excited to join. I'm remote (PST) and will be async-first during week 1. Happy to read existing runbooks and meet the runtime, sres, and devex teams.

Evan (Eng Manager - Runtime): Timeline for the senior SRE backfill: internal apps open through Apr 10, interviews Apr 17–24, final decision by May 1 so we have overlap with Priya. If you want to recommend folks, DM me or drop names here.

Lina (Talent): Internal referral form: <https://company.internal/hiring/runtime-relief> — please fill out by EOD Apr 9. I'll kick off the Greenhouse loop after the internal window closes.

Ben (Eng Lead, Observability): Quick question on reporting: will observability engineers move under Priya or remain matrixed? I want to lock down owner for the alerting playbooks.

Maya (People Ops): Short answer: observability engineers remain matrixed to Eng Platform, but Priya will have a dotted-line for SLOs, runbook ownership, and incident postmortems during the transition. Full org chart and a short decision tree are in the transition doc.

Priya Natarajan: I'll set up 30-min intro office hours during the week of May 6 — open slots for 1:1s and live walkthroughs of the infra. Please send me the top 3 pain points & the dashboards you rely on.

Anita (People Ops Bot): Anita created a calendar: "Priya intro — 1:1s" and invited runtime, sres, and devex teams.

Chris (SRE): We should prioritize the cache-eviction bug — it's contributing to intermittent p99 spikes on hosted inference. I can take interim ownership for observability and run the on-call for the next 3 weeks if that helps.

Evan (Eng Manager - Runtime): Thanks Chris — yes please. We'll pair you with Priya on onboarding and handoff sessions.

Maya (People Ops): Action summary:
- Lina: internal app link + referral form (due Apr 9)
- Evan: interview calendar + hiring rubric (internal then external)
- Priya: intro office hours week of May 6
- Chris: interim observability lead for next 3 weeks (confirmed)
- People Ops: transition doc + org chart attached in thread (see doc below)

If you have candidates to recommend or questions about ownership, @Maya and @Lina are the go-to. Thanks all — please react with :thumbsup: if you saw this.

title: #eng-platform: 1741073409
preview:
Mina Haddad: circling back on the `recommended` alias semantics. i’m still worried we’ll accidentally create “silent upgrades” if a customer sets `quantization_profile_ref=recommended` and we treat it as a dynamic pointer.

Ethan Park: +1, we need to lock this down before we finalize config service behavior. question: do we *ever* want the stored route config to be `recommended`?

Jordan Lee: if we store the alias, determinism is gone unless we define a specific resolution moment. and even then audit diffs get weird (config unchanged, behavior changed).

Benji Okafor: customer ergonomics: lots of folks will ask for “just keep me on the best profile”. but i agree we can’t do silent behavior changes for v1.

Priya Natarajan: we promised opt-in and reversible. “no customer action” changes violate that. also enterprise change control teams will block us.

Chloe Martin: from Console POV: the button label “Enable recommended” implies it follows updates. we should either change copy or make it explicit it pins.

Ravi Menon: we also have the multi-region issue: “recommended” could be different between H100 and A10 pools. if route config is just alias, policy eval has to resolve per region/gpu class, which makes it hard to reason about rollbacks.

Quentin Harris: model registry can expose `recommended_profile_id` per (model_id, variant, gpu_class, context_bucket) but i really don’t want callers depending on “recommended changes == live behavior changes”. we’ll be updating that field as we publish/rollback.

Naomi Feldman: audit/RBAC angle: if a profile changes under the hood, who is the “actor”? we’d need audit events for “system updated recommended pointer” and also a linkage to which customer routes were affected. that’s a lot for v1.

Ethan Park: proposal: API accepts `recommended` as *input only*; config service resolves it at write-time and persists the concrete `profile_id`. runtime/policy never sees alias.

Jordan Lee: +1. also store resolution metadata in the audit event (requested_ref, resolved_profile_id, resolution_scope). but route config itself stays concrete.

Benji Okafor: ok so semantics are: 
- `recommended` = “resolve now” 
- it does NOT auto-follow updates 
we should return something in the write response like `resolved_quant_profile_id` so it’s not surprising.

Chloe Martin: we can add UI copy: “Pins the current recommended version. Future recommended updates won’t apply unless you change this setting.” and maybe show the resolved id inline (with a “copy” action).

Ravi Menon: and if the pinned id becomes deprecated/blocked later, policy engine should fallback to baseline + emit `fallback_reason=deprecated_profile` and Console should surface a warning banner.

Priya Natarajan: yes. but importantly: *no automatic repointing* to the new recommended. fallback only, and customer chooses what to do next.

Quentin Harris: we can support that with registry states: `status=active|deprecated|blocked` and policy can treat `blocked` as hard fail (or forced fallback) depending on rollout mode. but i’d prefer forced fallback for safety.

Naomi Feldman: audit event should capture both the pinned id and any subsequent fallback events. esp for FalconBank-ish customers asking “prove nothing changed without approval”.

Ethan Park: sounds like consensus. action items:
- i’ll update config service doc + implementation: accept `recommended` input, persist resolved id only
- Chloe: update Console copy to “Pin recommended” or add explicit note
- Jordan/Ravi: confirm policy behavior for deprecated/blocked profile ids (fallback + metric)
- Benji: update OpenAPI + SDK notes so clients don’t assume it’s dynamic

Chloe Martin: ack, will add to DES/ENG notes. we’ll also add a tooltip “Want auto-updates? Not supported in v1” so we don’t overpromise.

Benji Okafor: i’ll also add an API error message for folks trying to PATCH with `recommended` and expecting it to stick: response includes `stored_quantization_profile_ref=<id>`.

Priya Natarajan: perfect. “no silent profile changes” is the line we should repeat in docs + sales.

Jordan Lee: i’ll append this to ADR language: “Aliases MAY be accepted at write time for convenience but MUST NOT be persisted; system will not silently change pinned profiles.”
